multi_path_vars.description: The primary search path for executable files.
Directories searched for header files (.h, .hpp) during compilation.
Directories for external or third-party header files.
Directories searched for library files during the linking phase.
Search paths for metadata files and assembly references.
Augments the default search path for Python module files.
Augments the library search path for Ruby modules.
List of locations where Ruby looks for installed gems.
List of directories to search for Perl modules.
Directories where the dynamic linker searches for shared libraries (.so).
Directories where the dynamic linker searches for dynamic libraries (.dylib).
Directories to search for manual pages.
Directories containing metadata and library references specific to the Windows SDK.
Internal variable used to restore the system path state.
Path to the directory containing php.ini file (can be a list in some configs).

single_path_vars.description: The current user's home directory.
The current user's home directory.
The root directory of the Java installation.
The directory where the Go SDK itself is installed.
The workspace root for Go projects.
Location of Cargo's configuration, cache, and binaries (usually ~/.cargo).
Location where rustup stores toolchains and data (usually ~/.rustup).
Path to the currently active Python virtual environment.
Root directory for user-site packages.
The primary directory where new gems are installed.
Directory where Node Version Manager (nvm) is installed.
The directory where Deno stores its cache and configuration.
The root directory of the Android SDK installation.
The root directory of the Flutter SDK.
Directory for Cabal configuration and packages.
The root directory for the vcpkg C++ library manager.
Directory where applications store user-specific data.
Directory where applications store user-specific data.
The directory used for temporary files.
The directory used for temporary files.
Standard location for user-specific configuration files.
Standard location for user-specific data files.
Standard location for user-specific cache data.
The directory containing the core operating system files.
The directory containing the core operating system files.
The installation directory for the Chocolatey package manager.
